# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Twilio\Rest\Client;
use Twilio\Exceptions\TwilioException;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class TwilioSmsService
{
    protected Client $twilioClient;
    protected string $twilioFromNumber;

    /**
     * TwilioSmsService constructor.
     * Initializes the Twilio client with credentials from configuration.
     *
     * @throws \RuntimeException If Twilio credentials are not properly configured.
     */
    public function __construct()
    {
        $sid = config('services.twilio.sid');
        $token = config('services.twilio.token');
        $from = config('services.twilio.from');

        if (empty($sid) || empty($token) || empty($from)) {
            Log::error('Twilio credentials are not fully configured. Check TWILIO_SID, TWILIO_TOKEN, and TWILIO_FROM in your .env and services.php.');
            throw new \RuntimeException('Twilio service is not configured correctly. Missing SID, Token, or From number.');
        }

        $this->twilioClient = new Client($sid, $token);
        $this->twilioFromNumber = $from;
    }

    /**
     * Sends an SMS message using Twilio.
     *
     * @param string $to The recipient's phone number (e.g., "+1234567890"). Must be E.164 format.
     * @param string $message The body of the SMS message.
     * @param string|null $from The sender's Twilio phone number. Defaults to the configured number if null. Must be E.164 format.
     * @return bool True if the message was sent successfully, false otherwise.
     */
    public function sendSms(string $to, string $message, ?string $from = null): bool
    {
        try {
            $fromNumber = $from ?? $this->twilioFromNumber;

            Log::info("Attempting to send SMS to {$to} from {$fromNumber}. Message: '{$message}'");

            $this->twilioClient->messages->create(
                $to,
                [
                    'from' => $fromNumber,
                    'body' => $message,
                ]
            );

            Log::info("SMS successfully sent to {$to}.");
            return true;
        } catch (TwilioException $e) {
            Log::error("Failed to send SMS to {$to}: " . $e->getMessage(), [
                'code' => $e->getCode(),
                'more_info' => $e->getMoreInfo(),
                'status_code' => $e->getStatusCode(),
            ]);
            return false;
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            Log::error("An unexpected error occurred while sending SMS to {$to}: " . $e->getMessage());
            return false;
        }
    }
}

// config/services.php
// Add the following entry to your config/services.php file:

//    'twilio' => [
//        'sid' => env('TWILIO_SID'),
//        'token' => env('TWILIO_TOKEN'),
//        'from' => env('TWILIO_FROM'), // Your Twilio phone number, e.g., '+15017122661'
//    ],


// .env
// Add these variables to your .env file:

// TWILIO_SID=ACxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
// TWILIO_TOKEN=your_auth_token_here
// TWILIO_FROM=+1xxxxxxxxxx
